text:
The second day of the ParisOlympic Gamesproved eventful and thrilling in equal measure.

Yasmin Harper and Scarlett Mew Jensen got the ball rollingfor Team GB on Saturday, clinching bronze in the women’s 3m Synchro event, beforeAnna Henderson followed up with a surprise silverin the women’s individual time trial.

Team GB won two more medals on Day Two, Adam Peaty winning silver in the 100m breaststroke and Kimberley Woods clinching bronze in the kayak singles.

Peaty was going for a third straightOlympic swimminggold but fell agonisingly short, just 0.02 seconds behind Italy’s surprise winner Nicolo Martinenghi.

The 29-year-old Briton was aiming to become the second man after Michael Phelps to win the same individual event at three Games in a row.

Elsewhere, US superstar Simone Biles looked back to her formidable best in the gymnastics, whilecontroversial Dutch beach volleyballer Steven van de Velde, a convicted sex offender, was booed during his first outing.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

We’ll be back to cover all that and more tomorrow morning. Goodnight!

Archery(men’s team),artistic gymnastics(men’s team),canoe slalom(men’s C1),diving(men’s synchro 10m platform),equestrian(eventing jumping team, eventing jumping individual),fencing(men foil, women sabre),judo(W -57kg, M -73kg),mountain bike(men’s cross-country),shooting(men’s and women’s 10m air rifle),skateboard(men’s street),swimming(women’s 400m individual medley, men’s 200m free, men’s 100m back, women’s 100m breaststroke, women’s 200m free).

Team GB stars to keep an eye on:

The photographer fluffed his lines just as Adam Peaty walked past ð¤ð#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/dUqcPtieCJ

âNot just a role model for British sport, but a role model for ð¦ð¸ð»ðµð­ sportâ ð@Iwanrunnerwith the highest praise for Team GBâs Adam Peaty following his silver medal at#Paris2024ðpic.twitter.com/advZiLhoJ0

In other news, a commentator at the Olympics has beenremoved after making a sexist remark about Australia’s female swimmers.

An emotional Adam Peaty has spoken out after coming so close to yet another gold medal.

Here’s what the Team GB legend said.

So so close.Itâs silver for Adam Peaty in the menâs 100m breaststrokeÂ ð¥#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/lAdqFBKFo0

Italy’s Nicolo Martinenghi claims an unexpected gold, with Peaty sharing the silver medal with American Nic Fink – who recorded the exact same time, 59.05 seconds.

Germany’s Melvin Imoudu bagged bronze, while China’s Qin Haiyang, one of the favourites, only seventh.

Wow, that was close! It all came down to the final stroke but Peaty was denied gold by the barest of all margins.

It’s almost time for Team GB star Adam Peaty to take to the pool.

He already has five Olympic medals to his name – three gold, two silver – and has a big chance of adding to his tally in the next few minutes…

Understandably, Andy Murray’s mum Judy thought her son was crashing out of the Olympics and his last competitive tournament.

‘I was mentally working out which flight I would get home tonight,’ she told BBC Sport.

‘But you can never write him off.’

But the British legend lives to fight another day after a miraculous comeback…

Understandably, Andy Murray’s mum Judy thought her son was crashing out of the Olympics and his last competitive tournament.

‘I was mentally working out which flight I would get home tonight,’ she told BBC Sport.

‘But you can never write him off.’

But the British legend lives to fight another day…

Remember the ‘naked blue man’ from the Olympics opening ceremony?

Well he’sspoken out and addressed the controversy over his role in the showpiece event.

Elsewhere, fanssuspect one interestingly-named athlete might have a bit of a advantage in the Paris 2024 rowing event.



Team GB could have more reason to cheer in the next hour as swimmer Adam Peaty goes in the men’s 100m breaststroke final at 20:44 BST.

And just look what it means to the Team GB duo…

Wow.Andy Murray and Dan Evans comeback from 4-9 (!!!!) in the deciding match tiebreak to beat Nishikori/Daniel 2-6, 7-6(5), 11-9 and reach the second round in#Paris2024.What an atmosphere.Long moment at the net between Andy and Kei (after this video)pic.twitter.com/wrbtANCAsI

A reminder that Andy Murray and Great Britain team-mate Dan Evans lost the first set 6-2. They were outplayed.

But Team GB fought back superbly to take the next set in a tense tie-break.

That led to a tie-break decider and, again, it looked like Japan were about to send Murray into retirement.

They led 9-4 before wasting five match points and six in a row, Team GB producing an epic comeback to keep Murray’s tennis career alive.

There was some evidence of nerves from the Japanese team – a double-fault aiding Team GB’s comeback – but Murray and Evans produced so much quality when it mattered most.

Reacting to Team GB’s victory, former British number one Sam Smith said on BBC: ‘Would you believe it? Yes!

‘We have been here before, many times, over the past decade. Goodness me!’

Would you believe it?! Japan squander FIVE match points in a dramatic tie-break decider and Team GB are somehow through. Incredible.

Team GB are still going as Andy Murray and Dan Evans level it up with Japan at 1-1.

Taro Daniel and Kei Nishikori looked certain to send Murray into retirement, but the Brits have fought back!

description: Follow latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 2 (July 28) schedule: badminton, swimming and gymnastics qualifying
